title: Moorea Boat Rental Guide: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Moorea is the best way to explore its crystalline lagoon and dramatic peaks. Depart from Vaiare Wharf or the Sofitel Marina and expect to pay from €250 for a half-day motorboat to €1,500 for a crewed catamaran. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Moorea?

Expect to pay between €250 and €1,500 per day depending on the boat type and duration. Shared tours start at €80 per person, while a small motorboat for six costs around €350 for four hours. Catamarans for a full day range from €800 to €1,200, and crewed yachts with a skipper start at €1,000.

To compare verified boat options in Moorea, visit boat rental Moorea on BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Moorea

Most boat rentals depart from the main harbours and marinas around the island. Vaiare Wharf is the busiest port, handling ferries and charter boats. The Sofitel Marina at Temae offers protected moorings and is ideal for launching.

Other good starting points include the privately run quay at the Hilton Moorea Lagoon Resort, the village of Papetoai with its shallow ramp, and the bay of Haapiti on the west coast, close to the pass out to the open sea. Cook's Bay and Opunohu Bay both have small jetties for pick-ups.

Which boat to rent in Moorea

  1. Small motorboat (5, 6 m), best for couples or small families exploring the lagoon; fits 4, 6 guests; from €250 half-day.
  2. Catamaran (40, 50 ft), ideal for day charters to Tiahura motu; up to 12 guests; from €800 full-day.
  3. Sailboat (30, 40 ft), perfect for those who want a relaxed cruise with wind assistance; sleeps 4, 6; from €400 half-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (Fairline 43, Azimut 50), for exclusive trips with a skipper; fits 8, 10 guests; from €1,200 half-day. See the Fairline listing on BoatHire24 for details.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Moorea?

You need a valid boating licence for any vessel over 6 hp unless you charter a boat with a skipper. French Polynesia enforces the same licence requirements as mainland France, so an ICC or national licence is accepted. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book.

When sailing from Haapiti to the outer reef, watch for the strong ‘maramu’ wind that picks up from May to October; it can make the pass tricky for novice skippers.

At a glance

  • Price band: €250, €1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to October (dry season)
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hrs) to full-day (8 hrs)
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

In 2026, BoatHire24 continues to simplify boat rentals with transparent pricing and verified operators.

Questions about boat rental in Moorea

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Moorea?

You can rent a small outboard motorboat (under 6 hp) without a licence, but for larger boats you must have a valid licence or hire a skipper.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Moorea?

The dry season from June to October offers calm seas and reliable trade winds, but the lagoon remains protected year-round.

How far is it from Moorea to Tahiti by boat?

The distance from Vaiare to Papeete is about 17 nautical miles across the Sea of the Moon, a 45-minute ferry ride.

Can I anchor overnight in Moorea lagoon?

Yes, overnight anchoring is permitted in designated areas such as Opunohu Bay and off the Sofitel Marina, though you must avoid coral beds.

Ready to explore Moorea from the water? Book or list your boat on BoatHire24, the worldwide marketplace for boat rentals.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Moorea and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
